source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Garden Banks Block 427

event.top_event: Hand injury from air hoist cable entanglement
actions_taken: All stop signal given by employee on fourth level
Riser insert lowered to deck to free injured finger
Injured party reported to Rig Medic
Tetanus shot and over-the-counter medication administered
Injured party evacuated onshore for medical evaluation
Orthopedic surgery performed to repair fracture
BSEE onsite investigation conducted on 14 January 2021
event.summary: On 10 January 2021 at 15:35 hours, a welder acting as a Rigger sustained a crush injury to his right index finger when he placed his hand on an air hoist cable during lifting operations of a riser insert in the Starboard riser bay hold area. The finger was drawn up and squeezed between the cable and sheave. The injured party was evacuated and diagnosed with a hairline fracture requiring orthopedic surgery. The incident was caused by improper hand placement and contributed to by supervisor failure to request crane use, crew failure to stop the job, and inadequate task assessment in safety documentation.

hazards.name: Pinch point between cable and sheave

threats.name: Hand contact with moving air hoist cable
Inadequate work method selection (air hoist vs crane)

consequences.name: Crush injury to right index finger

controls.name: Use of hands-free equipment for riser positioning
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hand contact with moving cable

supporting_text: Two other employees were positioned on the fourth level, using hands-free equipment, pushed the riser insert away from the scaffolding. The IP located on the fifth level was not using hands-free equipment.

controls.name: Supervisor request to use rig crane for lifting
controls.barrier_role: Select safer lifting method to prevent hand contact

supporting_text: The Supervisor used the rig crane to lift the riser inserts on the Port side without incident, but he did not request to lift the riser inserts on the Starboard side with the crane.
failure of the Supervisor to request using the rig crane instead of the air hoist to lift the riser inserts that was performed previously on the Port side without incident

controls.name: Job stop procedure when unsafe hand placement observed
controls.barrier_role: Stop work when hazardous hand placement detected

supporting_text: the crew failed to stop the job when the IP placed his hand on the air hoist cable
One of the employees on the fourth level signaled an all stop and the riser insert was lowered to the deck, freeing the IP's right index finger.

controls.name: Permit to Work and Job Safety Analysis task assessment
controls.barrier_role: Identify critical lift hazards and controls in planning

supporting_text: the tasks and hazards were not properly addressed in the Permit to Work and the Job Safety Analysis since the documents did not categorize lifting riser inserts as a critical lift; however, the Lift Plan did categorize it as a critical lift
the job task was not categorized as a critical lift as stated in the Lift Plan

controls.name: Immediate response and medical treatment
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through rapid response

supporting_text: One of the employees on the fourth level signaled an all stop and the riser insert was lowered to the deck, freeing the IP's right index finger.

The IP reported to the Rig Medic and was observed to have sustained a small abrasion on his right index finger. The Rig Medic gave the IP a tetanus shot and over the counter medication for his injury.
On 11 January 2021, the IP was sent in onshore on a crew change flight for a medical evaluation.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
